supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on Google for significant portion of traffic generation through pay-per-click marketing; dependence on third-party computer systems including GDSs and central reservation systems; reliance on payment card processors and networks; dependence on third-party service providers for call centers and customer service
supply_chain.risks: Google could change search algorithms or pricing negatively impacting traffic; third-party systems failures could prevent bookings; payment processor disruptions could impact transactions; concentration risk with major travel service providers; potential withdrawal of major accommodation providers; cybersecurity vulnerabilities in third-party systems

regulatory_risks: EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) compliance and potential fines up to 4% of global revenues
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and state consumer privacy laws with private right of action
Digital Markets Act (DMA) - designated as gatekeeper with additional compliance obligations
Digital Services Act (DSA) - Booking.com designated as Very Large Online Platform (VLOP)
EU Package Travel Directive requirements for travel packages and linked arrangements
Competition law investigations by national competition authorities regarding parity arrangements
Spanish competition authority fine and business practice restrictions
Payment Services Directive 2 and 3 compliance requirements
Digital services taxes in multiple jurisdictions (1.5% to 10% of revenues)
Alternative accommodation regulations and local registration requirements
Insurance distribution regulations for travel-related insurance products
Data localization requirements in certain countries
Travel agent registration requirements in various jurisdictions
ePrivacy Directive and cookie consent requirements
AI regulation including EU Artificial Intelligence Act
Money transmission and payment processing licensing requirements
Export control regulations for payment services expansion
Anti-corruption laws (US Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, UK Bribery Act)
Sanctions compliance (US, EU, UN sanctioned countries and persons)
Tax compliance including GILTI, BEAT, and international tax reform (OECD)
Travel transaction taxes disputes in multiple US jurisdictions
Works council consultation requirements in Europe

sanction_risks: US, EU, or UN sanctioned country mandates restricting business operations
Booking made at property potentially owned by party blocked under Executive Order 13224
Risk of conducting business with sanctioned parties through payment systems
Potential violations impacting ability to conduct business in certain jurisdictions
